title: Suspended sentences for illegal waste site boss and landlord
body: "Charles Willard, 41, of Priors Lease Lane, Hambrook and Callan Wood, 35, of Town Cross Avenue, Bognor Regis received suspended sentences for their roles in an illegal waste transfer station at a site in Chichester.   Willard operated a waste transfer station without environmental permits, while Wood allowed the site to operate within Appledram Business Park, which is owned by the company he managed.   Anyone wishing to carry out a waste operation can only do so in accordance with an environmental permit granted under the Environmental Permitting Regulations unless a waste operation is exempt.   For an operation to be exempt, an ‘exemption’ must be applied for, and certain conditions need to be met. Neither an exemption nor a permit was in place for the activities taking place on site.   Guilty pleas to running a site and letting it happen on owned land Charles Willard pleaded guilty on two charges of operating a waste site without a permit, and was given 16 weeks prison sentence suspended for 1 year.   Callan Wood pleaded guilty to one charge of allowing the operation of a waste site without an environmental permit, and was given an 18 weeks prison sentence suspended for 1 year.    Both men were banned from being company directors for 2 years, and each ordered to pay a share of the costs and a victim surcharge.   Willard’s companies Hello Skips Ltd and Eko Hire Ltd pleaded guilty to one offence each of operating a waste site without a permit.   Wood’s company CW Bikes Ltd pleaded guilty to one charge of allowing the operation of a waste site without an environmental permit.   Fines, costs and victim surcharges amounted to more than £44,000, split between the two men and three micro companies.   ‘We will press for the strongest penalties’  Photographs received from Sussex Police via drone footage, show the scale of the waste operations. An Environment Agency spokesperson said:     Illegal waste processing sites pose a threat to the environment and undermine legitimate waste businesses, and we will continue to press for the strongest possible penalties.   The Environment Agency will also prosecute landowners who turn a blind eye to illegal waste sites on their land.   If anyone suspects that waste crime is taking place in your community, contact our 24/7 hotline on 0800 80 70 60 or report it anonymously to Crimestoppers on 0800 555 111.   Agency flags illegal waste operation to landowner Appledram Business Park in Chichester is owned by CW Bikes Limited, managed by director Callan Wood. The site is divided into various plots which CW Bikes Limited rents out to various businesses.   On 28 July 2022 Environment Agency officers attended the site following a routine visit to another permitted yard on the site. They discovered an unpermitted waste transfer station: a site that collects and sorts waste before sending for disposal.   The site was estimated to be 50 metres by 30 metres in size with a six-foot fence running along three sides of the yard.  Large mounds of construction waste, soil, and...
